observations: Largest gain came from eval-1 and eval-3 where new assertions explicitly check mandatory tdd/code-simplifier/independent-review language.
Eval-2 remains non-discriminating for the new requirements: both configurations scored 8/9 and both missed the same validation-checkpoint assertion.
Eval-0 is now saturated (both 5/5), so it validates baseline hygiene but contributes little to separation.
New eval-3 is discriminating and directly measures the code-simplifier requirement gap between versions.
recommendations: In the next iteration, revise eval-2 prompt and assertion 9 so the task explicitly asks for validation checkpoints tied to implementation/review workflow, reducing ambiguity.
Add a second medium-feature implementation eval focused on post-implementation review sequencing to increase sensitivity to quality-phase behavior changes.
